governance_styles.description: 9 explicit statuses form a directed lifecycle with manual transition triggers. 'proposed' and 'ready' require planner assignment; 'running' requires executor dispatch; 'waiting_review' and 'verified' reflect human or acceptance-node gating; 'failed/deprecated' are terminal.
evidence: src/devforge/graph/builder.py — run_cycle() drives status transitions via node sequence, not auto-promotion

governance_styles.description: resolve_retry_action() applies deterministic guardrails (attempt_count >= max_attempts → final_fail) first. Only when rules pass does control flow to llm_retry_decider() for contextual routing (switch_executor/requeue/block/replan/final_fail).
evidence: src/devforge/planning/retry_policy.py:8-47 — rule-based guardrail: attempt_count, seam/contract signals, executor fallback list
src/devforge/planning/retry_decision.py — llm_retry_decider() called only after rule guardrail passes

governance_styles.description: Seam must reach 'frozen' status before parallel implementation is allowed. verify_seam_compliance() enforces this gate by checking acceptance criteria coverage and deviation keywords in executor summaries.
evidence: src/devforge/seams/verifier.py:46-144 — verify_seam_compliance() — compliance checked against wp_results summaries
PLAN.md:424-445 — seam workflow: define→generate→review→freeze→allow parallel implementation

governance_styles.description: Requirement changes enter as RequirementEvent records (type: add/modify/remove/reprioritize) with patch_status lifecycle (recorded→analyzing→planned→applied/rejected). apply_requirement_events() performs incremental graph patches, not full rewrite.
evidence: PLAN.md:416-419 — 'Requirement change is a structured event, not informal chat drift. The engine should patch graph state incrementally.'

governance_styles.description: select_workset() applies priority ordering + seam freeze gate + dependency readiness filter. Batch selection explicitly avoids work packages with unresolved input artifacts or unfrozen seam dependencies.
evidence: src/devforge/scheduler/workset_selector.py — select_workset() with priority sort and dependency filter
PLAN.md:693-707 — workset selection rules: prefer frozen contracts, avoid unresolved inputs and non-frozen seams

governance_styles.description: evaluate_acceptance() returns AcceptanceVerdict combining goal completion, closure density (ring-0/ring-1 task coverage), and work package results. On failure, analyze_gaps() generates RemediationPackage with LLM-attributed gap items.
evidence: src/devforge/state/acceptance.py — AcceptanceVerdict, GoalCheckResult, GapItem, ClosureDensityScore dataclasses

in_scope: Orchestration state machine (LangGraph meta-graph with 9 nodes)
Work package lifecycle and status transitions
Context pull manifest and URI resolution (project://, artifact://, memory://)
external: Executor-owned: prompt format construction, actual code writing/reading, test execution, output artifact content — ExecutorAdapter.normalize_result() is the only re-entry boundary
LLM provider-owned: model selection, token limits, API rate limiting — LLMClient Protocol abstracts OpenRouter and Google GenAI
Tool-owned: web search (Brave), image generation (fal.ai), UI prototyping (Google Stitch) — injected only into tool-enhanced node variants
OS/environment-owned: subprocess lifecycle, working directory paths, binary PATH discovery

stages: proposed — work package exists in plan but not yet ready for execution
ready — all input dependencies resolved, available for workset selection
running — dispatched to executor, SubmissionReceipt recorded
blocked — waiting on seam contract or external dependency
waiting_review — executor returned result, pending human or acceptance-node review
verified — acceptance criteria confirmed by acceptance node or human
failed — executor returned failure, retry routing exhausted
deprecated — invalidated by requirement patch or project restructuring
transitions: proposed → ready: execution_planner role or planning_and_shaping_node assigns dependencies and executor
ready → running: batch_dispatch node selects from ready_queue via select_workset() and calls adapter.dispatch()
running → waiting_review / completed / failed: batch_verification node normalizes ExecutorResult
waiting_review → verified: acceptance_and_gap_check node or human confirmation
failed → ready (requeue) / ready with new executor (switch_executor) / blocked / deprecated: resolve_retry_action() + llm_retry_decider()
any status → deprecated: apply_requirement_events() marks affected work packages on requirement change
any status → failed: max_attempts reached in resolve_retry_action() → final_fail

distribution_model.install: pip install devforge  OR  uv sync --extra dev
evidence: pyproject.toml — Python >=3.12, setuptools, uv; no payment or license enforcement code
README.md:18 — 'uv sync --extra dev' as the install command
